board.designation: 60A Common-Area + Exterior Lighting Panel — Sunset Plaza

circuits.designation: Fire pump controller dedicated tap (life-safety, NEC 695.4)

non_compliance_flags.message: NEC 695.4(A) requires the fire pump to be supplied by an independent source — a dedicated direct connection to the utility service, a dedicated on-site generator, or a dedicated feeder tap upstream of the MSP main service disconnect. Routing C05 fire pump branch through the CA-P common-area panelboard does NOT comply with NEC 695.4. RECOMMENDATION: re-route C05 as a dedicated tap from upstream of MSP-A main service disconnect, with its own fire pump controller per NEC 695.6 + dedicated transfer-switch arrangement. The 60A MCCB + 6 AWG Cu feeder remain correct sizing per NEC 695.7 voltage-drop requirements.

Bus rating concern: 60A intake busbar + 60A C05 fire pump branch leaves no headroom for other circuits when fire pump is at LRA. NEC 408.30 requires busbar rating ≥ sum of supply OCPDs. After C05 re-routing per the 695.4 flag above, CA-P busbar 60A is appropriate for the remaining ~7 kW common-area load.
GFCI 10mA Class A applied to common-area receptacles C04 per NEC 210.8(B)(7) outdoor + 210.8(B)(8) janitor/utility — outdoor maintenance receptacles are within scope.

assumptions: Diversity factor 0.9 applied per NEC 220.61 (common-area lighting + receptacles minimal diversity due to coincident operation).
Selectivity verification deferred (tool_call_pending) per WI3 — fault-level intent absent and engineer Ifault not declared.
Cable ampacity check assumes NEC Table 310.16 (THWN-2 in conduit, 30°C ambient): 14 AWG Cu = 20A; 12 AWG Cu = 25A; 10 AWG Cu = 35A; 6 AWG Cu = 65A. All In ≤ Iz.
EGC sizing per NEC 250.122 matched to OCPD ratings.
C01 parking lot lighting wired as 240V branch (line-to-line at 208V Y-connected supply) to handle the ~17A draw across 60m run with voltage-drop margin.
C03 corridor lighting treated as emergency_lighting voltage_class per NEC 700.6 — life-safety egress always-on lighting; NO GFCI per NEC 700.6.

C05 fire pump: 60A MCCB with 22 kA breaking capacity per NEC 695.4(B) requirement to coordinate with fire-pump locked-rotor current (cannot trip on motor inrush). Cable 6 AWG Cu 4-core for 3-phase + EGC.
C06 site security cameras + intercom intentionally NOT classified as emergency_lighting — security is non-life-safety; the security system has its own UPS backup at the equipment cabinet.

rationale.chat_summary: 60A 208Y/120V 3-phase NEMA Type 1 common-area + exterior lighting panel for Sunset Plaza. 6 circuits: parking lot LED (30A 240V), façade signage, corridor egress lighting (emergency_lighting class), common-area receptacles with GFCI, fire pump controller (NEC 695 life-safety), site security. CRITICAL FLAG: C05 fire pump as a branch off CA-P violates NEC 695.4 — fire pump requires dedicated independent source. Engineer should re-route C05 as a dedicated tap upstream of MSP-A main service disconnect.

sections.title: Board Classification
sections.summary: US NEMA Type 1 panelboard, 60A, 20-way common-area panel.

decisions.label: NEMA Type 1 enclosure
decisions.summary: Indoor general purpose; landlord service room.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 208Y/120V 3-phase 4-wire, 60A 2-pole feeder from MSP-A. Common-area + exterior lighting + landlord-managed loads. Ze 0.12Ω.

decisions.label: 60A 2-pole feeder
decisions.summary: Smaller than tenant feeders — common-area load is light vs tenant retail load.
decisions.rule: NEC tenant feeder sizing per NEC 220 load calculation

sections.title: Busbar Sizing
sections.summary: 60A busbar matches feeder rating, IcW 10 kA. Common-area load (excluding fire pump after re-routing) ≈ 7 kW ~ 20A diversified. After NEC 695.4 corrective re-route of C05, 60A busbar has 65% headroom.

decisions.label: 60A busbar IcW 10 kA
decisions.summary: Sized to match feeder OCPD at MSP-A.

sections.title: Way Assignment
sections.summary: 7 ways used: 5 × single-pole MCBs (W1-W4, W7) + 1 × 2-pole MCCB (W5-W6) for fire pump. 13 ways spare.

sections.title: OCPD Selection
sections.summary: Branch circuits sized per NEC 240. Parking lot 30A on 10 AWG (35A ampacity). Common-area 20A on 12 AWG (25A). Security 15A on 14 AWG (20A). Fire pump 60A on 6 AWG (65A ampacity). All In ≤ Iz pass.

decisions.label: 60A Type D MCCB on fire pump
decisions.summary: C05 fire pump motor needs Type D curve + 22 kA Icu MCCB. NEC 695.5 prohibits any OCPD that would trip on motor inrush — coordinate with manufacturer.

sections.title: RCD Strategy
sections.summary: NEC 210.8(B) commercial GFCI applied to C04 common-area receptacles (janitor + outdoor maintenance — outdoor + readily accessible). C01 + C02 lighting circuits no GFCI (lighting, no receptacle). C03 corridor lighting NO GFCI per NEC 700.6 life-safety. C05 fire pump NO GFCI per NEC 695 life-safety (would defeat purpose). C06 security intentionally NOT life-safety class — no GFCI required.

decisions.label: GFCI on C04 only
decisions.summary: Only readily-accessible outdoor / janitor receptacles are within NEC 210.8(B) scope.
decisions.rule: NEC 210.8(B) commercial GFCI + NEC 700.6 + NEC 695 exemptions

sections.title: Cable Selection
sections.summary: THWN-2 copper in EMT conduit. 6 AWG Cu 4-core for fire pump (3-phase + EGC) handles LRA per NEC 695.7. 10 AWG for parking lot 30A run at 60m — voltage drop within NEC 215.2(A)(1) 3% recommendation.

sections.title: Selectivity Verification
sections.summary: 6 cascade pairs (MSP-A → C01..C06) emitted as tool_call_pending. fault-level intent absent; engineer Ifault not declared. C05 fire pump cascade pair has special significance — NEC 695 requires the fire-pump OCPD to NOT trip during a fault upstream until the motor de-energizes (NEC 695.5(C)).

sections.title: Compliance
sections.summary: NON-COMPLIANT as drawn. CRITICAL: C05 fire pump branch from common-area panelboard violates NEC 695.4(A) (dedicated independent source required). HIGH: 60A intake + 60A fire pump branch leaves no busbar headroom for other circuits at LRA. INFO: GFCI scope on C04 per NEC 210.8(B). Engineer must re-route C05 before construction.
